data.title: Compass, Anywhere Real Estate avoided deal probe amid Justice Dept. rift - WSJ
data.description:
Justice Department antitrust officials wanted to investigate the $1.6 billion merger of Compass Inc. (COMP) and Anywhere Real Estate, but were overruled by senior officials who felt the deal should be approved.

Gail Slater, the head of the DOJ's antitrust division, wanted an extended review of the real estate brokerage deal, according to a WSJ report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Compass and its lawyers appealed above her, to the office of Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche, saying that any concerns could be addressed without an investigation. Blanche's office agreed. 

Compass and Anywhere declined to comment to the WSJ. A spokeswoman for Blanche said the department "complied with its obligations" under antitrust law to review the deal.

Compass (COMP) and Anywhere closed their deal on Friday.

data.title: Goldman Sachs cuts iHeartMedia to Sell as digital shift accelerates
data.description:
As advertisers migrate towards digital platforms and with audio consumption declining, iHeartMedia (IHRT) was downgraded to Sell at Goldman Sachs, driving shares further into the red to a 2-month low.

The firm also lowered its target price for the stock by 12.5% to a street low of $3.50.

IHeartMedia (IHRT) operates in three segments: Multiplatform Group, which accounts for 60% of revenue and is the company’s traditional radio business; Digital Audio Group, which accounts for 30% of revenue; and Audio & Video Media Services Group, which accounts for the remaining 10%. Of the three, the Multiplatform Group has been on a downward trajectory amid a shift away from broadcast radio to internet audio.

“Globally, broadcast radio has been declining as audiences, notably younger ones, shifted to digital platforms. Further declines may be possible going forward due to audience attrition as legacy listeners age out of the market. Around 25% of America’s population is aged 50 and over, and it is likely, considering current consumer preferences, that this loss will not be replaced by an equally engaged younger audience,” says Seeking Alpha analyst LD Investments.

Adds Goldman, “the continued rollout of connected in-car infotainment systems and increased penetration of audio services will be headwinds to AM/FM audio engagement.”

Besides a shift away from terrestrial radio, Goldman also warns of the expansion of artificial intelligence in the category that will introduce levels of control, curation, and discoverability to audio that will “further incentivize listeners to migrate from broadcast to digital audio service which are better suited to deliver AI capabilities.”

This is also true for advertisers, giving them the ability to personalize, target and enhance the efficiency of their ad budgets.

Looking ahead to the two most important advertising catalysts in 2026 – the FIFA World Cup and mid-term elections – broadcast radio/iHeartMedia will still benefit from these events, but Goldman warns that the scope of that benefit will be narrower compared to other formats.

Lastly, with $5B of debt maturing in 2031, free cash flow generation presents another worry for investors.

Although the company can refinance this debt, “it remains to be seen whether management can exchange this debt at a better rate,” LD Investments adds.

“My base case scenario, therefore, assumes a step up in interest payments (as much as a 30% increase from current levels, i.e. over $350M) along with higher interest rates.”

iHeartMedia (IHTR) shares are down 4.4% on Friday.

data.title: AllianceBernstein sees steady growth in 2026, but flags rising volatility and policy risks
data.description:
AllianceBernstein’s global macro outlook for the first quarter of 2026 strikes a cautiously constructive tone, emphasizing steady economic expansion alongside rising sources of uncertainty. The firm expects global growth to continue through the coming quarters at a pace sufficient to support financial markets, even if it falls short of historical averages.

At the same time, the investment firm highlights meaningful downside risks emerging from both policy decisions and underlying economic conditions. In the United States, the Federal Reserve faces a particularly complex backdrop the firm noted. A gradually cooling labor market combined with inflation that remains stubbornly above target complicates the path forward, leaving policymakers with no clearly optimal choice. 

From a structural perspective, artificial intelligence ((AI)) remains a long-term positive for productivity, but the investment firm cautions that such gains typically materialize slowly. Markets, however, may be less willing to wait for tangible evidence in the form of sustained earnings growth, particularly given the elevated valuations across many AI-related assets. 

Looking ahead, AllianceBernstein expects market conditions to become more volatile in 2026 after an unusually calm second half of 2025. With asset prices already reflecting a significant amount of optimism, the firm advises investors to temper return expectations.

Even favorable economic developments, it notes, may not translate into outsized gains in an environment where much of the good news is already priced in.

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Insmed gains as preliminary 2025 revenue beats consensus
data.description:
Insmed (INSM) shares traded higher on Friday after the rare disease drug developer announced preliminary unaudited revenue for 2025, exceeding Wall Street forecasts ahead of a presentation at the upcoming Annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference.

The Bridgewater, New Jersey-based biotech expects its full-year revenue to reach $606.4M, indicating ~67% YoY growth compared to $522.0M in the consensus, thanks mainly to $453.0M of U.S. revenue, which grew ~78% YoY.

Its key revenue generators, Arikayce and Brinsupri, have generated $172.7M and $433.8M to the topline, respectively, with Brinsupri adding $144.6M during Q4, its full quarter of commercial rollout.

Meanwhile, Arikayce, indicated for mycobacterium avium complex ((MAC)) lung disease, exceeded the upper end of the company’s prior revenue guidance, Insmed (INSM) said.

As for upcoming catalysts, topline readouts from the company’s Phase 3 ENCORE study of Arikayce in newly diagnosed or recurrent MAC lung disease and the Phase 2b CEDAR study of Brensocatib in hidradenitis suppurativa are now expected in March or April and Q2 2026, respectively.  

Insmed’s (INSM) JPM presentation is scheduled to take place on Jan. 12 at 6:00 p.m. ET.

data.title: Bitcoin crosses $90K mark amid investor optimism despite recent slide
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) was trading in green on Friday morning, briefly crossing the $90K threshold, as the broader markets opened higher.

The stock market inched higher as the key jobs report came largely as expected. December nonfarm payrolls growth came in roughly in line, while the unemployment rate dipped to 4.4%.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) had been trading lower recently, with outflows from spot BTC exchange-traded funds swelling to the most since November.

However, the highest-profile crypto edged up by 0.64% on Friday morning after ARK Invest founder Cathie Wood pointed out that crypto remains strategic with U.S. President Trump approaching midterm elections.

Recently, Fundstrat Global Advisors co-founder Tom Lee had said that bitcoin (BTC-USD) could hit a fresh all-time high by the end of January 2026.

Other major tokens also showed improvement during Friday morning trading, with Ether (ETH-USD) +0.02%, Dogecoin (DOGE-USD) +1.24%, and Ripple USD (XRP-USD) +0.05%. 

Crypto-focused stocks showed mixed price movements, with Strategy (MSTR) -3.39% to $161.31, Coinbase Global (COIN) -0.93% to $243.30, MARA Holdings (MARA) +1.20% to $10.57, Bakkt (BKKT) +2.19% to $16.82, Gemini Space Station (GEMI) -2.07% to $10.86, and Bullish (BLSH) -0.10% to $39.26.

data.title: Olin slides after guiding Q4 adjusted EBITDA below expectations
data.description:
Olin (OLN) -2.3% in early trading Friday after forecasting Q4 2025 adjusted EBITDA of $67M, compared to its previous outlook of $110M-$130M, missing the $121M analyst consensus estimate, citing weaker than expected demand for pipeline chlorine as well as extended planned maintenance turnaround and unplanned downtime at its Freeport, Texas operations; however, the company said the site is back up and running at normal levels.

"While we believe investor expectations on 4Q results were below the consensus, given the near greater than 40% miss against Street numbers, despite the miss mostly related to operational issues at the Freeport site, we expect negative stock reaction," Citi analyst Patrick Cunningham said, adding that while Olin (OLN) did not provide forward commentary on Q1 expectations, weak demand likely points to downside to Q1 consensus.

data.title: No decision yet on Trump tariffs as Supreme Court starts issuing opinions
data.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court on Friday released its first full opinion of the term without addressing legal challenges to President Donald Trump's tariffs.

There had been a good chance that SCOTUS could rule on the legality of Trump's tariffs Friday, which was set as the decision day.

But the court could issue additional opinions in the next couple of weeks, with court justices returning from holiday recess.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Argentina repays U.S. swap draw, Treasury Secretary Bessent says
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Friday that Argentina fully repaid its limited draw on the swap facility with the United States. 

Bessent, in a post on X, also praised Argentine President Javier Milei, saying that he has continued to deliver with full force on the renewed mandate from the Argentine people for economic freedom.

"Argentina has successfully reaccessed financial markets and has made encouraging changes to its monetary and exchange rate policy framework," Bessent said.

Bessent also said the Exchange Stabilization Fund currently does not hold any pesos.

Earlier in the week, it was reported that Argentina's treasury was purchasing dollars that entered the local market from the privatization of hydroelectric power plants as it accumulated U.S. currency to pay on bonds due this week.

Earlier in October, the U.S. Treasury finalized a $20B currency swap framework with Argentina and directly purchased Argentine pesos (ARS:USD).

"With Argentina stabilized, the markets are now meeting the Argentine Republic’s financing needs under President Milei’s visionary leadership," Bessent added.

The country's benchmark, the S&P MERVAL Index (MERVAL) rose 1% at the time of writing, and the Global X MSCI Argentina ETF (NYSEARCA:ARGT), which provides U.S. investors with exposure to Argentine equities, was down 0.13%.

Argentina's economy has been battling challenges for the past few months. In April, the International Monetary Fund reached a preliminary agreement with Argentina on a $20 billion bailout.

data.title: WD-40 shares hit 52-week low after Q1 earnings miss
data.description:
WD-40 (WDFC) shares slid on Friday, hitting a 52-week low, as the company posted first-quarter earnings that missed expectations, with management noting that sales came in below long-term growth targets and that a slower start to fiscal 2026 had been anticipated.

"The softness was driven by timing factors within our marketing distributor network, not by a decline in end-user demand. All indicators point to a strong rebound later in the fiscal year," CFO Sara Hyzer said on the conference call as the company reaffirmed its guidance. 

Regarding the household divestiture, the company said it continues to advance the potential sale of its Americas home care and cleaning product brands, with its investment bank engaged in discussions with multiple prospective buyers.

While no transaction is assured, management said it remains optimistic and will provide updates as appropriate.

WD-40 (WDFC) said "unanticipated inflationary headwinds and other unforeseen events" may affect its view of fiscal year 2026, and in the event, it is unsuccessful in the divestiture of the Americas homecare and cleaning brands, the guidance would be positively impacted by approximately $12.5 million in net sales, $3.6 million in operating income, and $0.20 in diluted EPS on a full-year basis.

Jefferies maintained its Hold rating and $229.00 price target on the company, while DA Davidson has reiterated its Buy rating and $300.00 price target.

Shares in the American manufacturer of household and multi-use products were last down -7.78% at $187.66.

data.title: U.S. stocks mostly higher after key labor data comes largely in line
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were largely higher on Friday as the key jobs report came in line with expectations. The indexes are on track for weekly gains after a week of losses.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.2% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.3%, and the Dow (DJI) was flat.

On focus today, the December nonfarm payrolls growth came in roughly in line, while the unemployment rate dipped to 4.4%.

“Friday's weaker-than-expected jobs report showed that hiring continued to slow towards the end of 2025, capping a tough year for the labor market. Although the market is still questioning the soundness of the jobs data given the recent disruptions from the government shutdown, throughout 2025, the labor market faced many challenges, including uncertainties from tariffs to artificial intelligence,” said Dennis Follmer, CIO at Montis Financial.

“Higher for longer on interest rates has been the name of the game for an economy continuing to surprise to the upside. As long as that economic strength continues, the market’s expectations for rate cuts are likely to prove overly optimistic,” Follmer said.

Also, the January consumer sentiment index also came in slightly higher than expected and higher than December, according to the University of Michigan Survey of Consumers.

Over in the bond market, Treasury yields advanced across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.18%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.52%.

In addition, investors await the Supreme Court's decision on the legality of President Donald Trump's tariffs. According to the apex court's website, it might come on Friday.

Also, Trump said he has called off plans for a second wave of military attacks on Venezuela, citing the release of political prisioners and calling it "a very important and smart gesture."
